title: Elatostema rupestre
what_description: Elatostema rupestre is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, native to China and Southeast Asia. The plant is a perennial herb with a woody base, growing up to 60 cm tall, with a rosette of leaves that are ovate to lanceolate in shape, 2-10 cm long, and 1-4 cm wide, with a petiole up to 2 cm long and a serrated margin. The leaves are glabrous on both sides, with 3-5 pairs of lateral veins that are parallel to the midvein. The plant produces small, greenish-yellow flowers in a terminal panicle, 5-10 cm long, with 4-6 sepals and 4-6 petals, and a fruit that is a small, green, glabrous follicle, 2-3 mm long. The plant is found in rocky areas, typically at elevations between 1000-3000 meters.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Elatostema rupestre are inflammation and diabetes, which are effectively managed by its anti-inflammatory and hypoglycemic properties. Elatostema rupestre has also been shown to alleviate arthritis symptoms, including reduced joint pain and swelling, through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Additionally, hypertension can be controlled by the plant's vasodilatory properties, which help to lower blood pressure. Moreover, Elatostema rupestre has been found to exhibit antineoplastic activity against cancer, as well as exhibit antimicrobial properties that aid in digestive issues. Furthermore, the plant has been used to treat a range of conditions, including anxiety, gastroenteritis, asthma, skin conditions, infection, fever, rheumatism, gastrointestinal problems, skin problems, hepatitis, eczema, atherosclerosis, oxidative stress, and wound healing.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Elatostema rupestre are attributed to its ability to scavenge free radicals, exhibiting potent antioxidant activity that neutralizes oxidative stress and protects against cell damage. This property is complemented by its antimicrobial action, which has been demonstrated to inhibit the growth of various pathogens, including bacteria and fungi, through the action of its bioactive compounds. Additionally, the plant has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and alleviating pain in inflamed tissues, while its analgesic properties provide relief from acute pain and discomfort. Furthermore, Elatostema rupestre has been shown to possess antiviral activity, inhibiting the replication of certain viruses and offering a potential therapeutic approach for viral infections. The bioactive compounds responsible for these medicinal properties include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Elatostema rupestre are flavonoids and phenolic compounds including quercetin, kaempferol, and phenolic acids. These compounds have been identified as the primary contributors to the plant's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Quercetin and kaempferol, two key flavonoids, have been shown to exhibit significant free radical scavenging and anti-inflammatory activities, making them effective in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation. Additionally, tannins, a class of polyphenolic compounds, have been found in Elatostema rupestre, contributing to its antimicrobial and astringent properties. These compounds work together to provide the plant's therapeutic benefits, including its ability to protect against cell damage and promote wound healing.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Elatostema rupestre are made to utilize its medicinal properties effectively. A tincture is created by soaking the plant in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin to extract its bioactive compounds. Elatostema rupestre tea is prepared by infusing the leaves in hot water to release its volatile oils and flavonoids. Decoction is used to extract the plant's minerals and other insoluble compounds by boiling the leaves and stems in water. Additionally, a salve is made by mixing the plant's extract with a carrier oil to create a topical application for skin conditions.
side_effects_description: The possible side effect of using Elatostema rupestre are gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea and vomiting which can be induced due to the presence of anthraquinones, a type of glycoside that can irritate the intestinal tract, potentially leading to nausea and inducing further gastrointestinal distress, causing discomfort and dizziness in some individuals. This is because the anthraquinones can stimulate bowel movements, causing a sudden increase in the frequency and urgency of defecation, leading to dehydration and electrolyte imbalances if not properly managed. Moreover, the laxative properties of Elatostema rupestre can also cause a drop in blood pressure, resulting in dizziness and lightheadedness. The nausea can be due to the rapid emptying of the stomach, leading to a sensation of vomiting and potentially inducing actual vomiting in severe cases. It is essential to use Elatostema rupestre under medical supervision and follow the recommended dosage to minimize the risk of these side effects.

Elatostema rupestre has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Further studies are needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Elatostema rupestre as a medicinal treatment. Elatostema rupestre is not widely recognized as a medicinal plant in mainstream medicine, and more research is required to understand its full potential.
taxonomy: Elatostema rupestre is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. It belongs to the genus Elatostema, which comprises approximately 400 species of plants. Elatostema rupestre is native to mountainous regions and is often found growing in rocky areas. The plant is a type of nettle relative and is known for its small, inconspicuous flowers. As a member of the Urticaceae family, Elatostema rupestre is closely related to other plants such as poison ivy and stinging nettle.

intro: Elatostema rupestre, also known as 'St. Robert's weed', is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Elatostema rupestre include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Elatostema rupestre is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of a poultice or salve to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
